title: EASTER SUNDAY OF THE RESURRECTION OF THE LORD

entrance_antiphon.latin: Resurrexi, et adhuc tecum sum, alleluia: posuisti super me manum tuam, alleluia: mirabilis facta est scientia tua, alleluia, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: I have risen, and I am with you still, alleluia. You have laid your hand upon me, alleluia. Too wonderful for me, this knowledge, alleluia, alleluia.

entrance_antiphon.latin: Surrexit Dominus vere, alleluia. Ipsi gloria et imperium per universa aeternitatis saecula, alleluia, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: The Lord is truly risen, alleluia. To him be glory and power for all the ages of eternity, alleluia, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Pascha nostrum immolatus est Christus, alleluia; itaque epulemur in azymis sinceritatis et veritatis, alleluia, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: Christ our Passover has been sacrificed, alleluia; therefore let us keep the feast with the unleavened bread of purity and truth, alleluia, alleluia.

title: MONDAY WITHIN THE OCTAVE OF EASTER

entrance_antiphon.latin: Introduxit vos Dominus in terram fluentem lac et mel; ut lex Domini sit semper in ore vestro, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: The Lord has led you into a land flowing with milk and honey, that the law of the Lord may always be on your lips, alleluia.

entrance_antiphon.latin: Resurrexit Dominus a mortuis, sicus dixerat; exsultemus et laetemur omnes, quoniam ipse regnat in aeternum, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: The Lord has risen from the dead, as he said; let us all exult and rejoice, for he reigns for all eternity, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Christus resurgens ex mortuis iam non moritur, mors illi ultra non dominabitur, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: Christ, having risen from the dead, dies now no more; death will no longer have dominion over him, alleluia.

title: TUESDAY WITHIN THE OCTAVE OF EASTER

entrance_antiphon.latin: Aqua sapientae potavit eos; firmabitur in illis, et non flectetur, exaltabit eos in aeternum, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: He gave them the water of wisdom to drink; it will be made strong in them and will not be moved; it will raise them up for ever, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Si consurrexistis cum Christo, quae sursum sunt quaerite, ubi Christus est in dextera Dei sedens; quae sursum sunt sapite, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: If you have risen with Christ, seek the things that are above, where Christ is seated at the right hand of God; mind the things that are above, alleluia.

title: WEDNESDAY WITHIN THE OCTAVE OF EASTER

entrance_antiphon.latin: Venite, benedicti Patris mei, percipite regnum, quod vobis paratum est ab origine mundi, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: Come, you blessed of my Father; receive the kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Cognoverunt discipuli Dominum Iesum in fractione panis, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: The disciples recognized the Lord Jesus in the breaking of the bread, alleluia.

title: THURSDAY WITHIN THE OCTAVE OF EASTER

entrance_antiphon.latin: Victricem manum tuam, Domine, laudeverunt pariter, quia sapientia aperuit os mutum, et linguas infantium fecit disertas, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: They praised in unison your conquering hand, O Lord, for wisdom opened mouths that were mute and gave eloquence to the tongues of infants, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Populus acquisitionis, annuntiate virtutes eius, qui vos de tenebris vocavit in admirabile lumen suum, alleluia
communion_antiphon.english: O chosen people, proclaim the mighty works of him, who called you out of darkness into his wonderful light, alleluia.

title: FRIDAY WITHIN THE OCTAVE OF EASTER

entrance_antiphon.latin: Eduxit Dominus populum suum in spe, et inimicos eorum operuit mare, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: The Lord led his people in hope, while the sea engulfed their foes, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Dixit Iesus discipulis suis:Venite, prandete. Et accepit panem, et dedit eis, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: Jesus said to his disciples: Come and eat. And he took bread and gave it to them, alleluia.

title: SATURDAY WITHIN THE OCTAVE OF EASTER

entrance_antiphon.latin: Eduxit Dominus populum suum in exsultatione, et electos suos in laetitia, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: The Lord brought out his people with joy, his chosen ones with shouts of rejoicing, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Omnes qui in Christo baptizati estis, Christum induistis, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: All of you who have been baptized in Christ have put on Christ, alleluia.

title: SECOND SUNDAY OF EASTER (or of Divine Mercy)

entrance_antiphon.latin: Quasi modo geniti infantes, rationabile, sine dolo lac concupscite, ut in eo crescatis in salutem, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: Like newborn infants, you must long for the pure, spiritual milk, that in him you may grow to salvation, alleluia.

entrance_antiphon.latin: Accipite iucunditatem gloriae vestrae, gratias agentes Deo, qui vos ad caelestia regna vocavit, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: Receive the joy of your glory, giving thanks to God, who has called you into the heavenly kingdom, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Mitte manum tuam, et cognosce loca clavorum, et noli esse incredulus, sed fidelis, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: Bring your hand and feel the place of the nails, and do not be unbelieving but believing, alleluia.

title: MONDAY In the Second Week of Easter

entrance_antiphon.latin: Christus resurgens ex mortuis iam non moritur, mors illi ultra non dominabitur, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: Christ, having risen from the dead, dies now no more; death will no longer have dominion over him, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Stetit Iesus in medio discipulorum suorum, et dixit eis: Pax vobis, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: Jesus stood in the midst of his disciples and said to them: Peace be with you, alleluia.

title: TUESDAY In the Second Week of Easter

entrance_antiphon.latin: Gaudeamus et exsultemus et demus gloriam Deo, quoniam regnavit Dominus Deus noster omnipotens, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: Let us rejoice and be glad and give glory to God, for the Lord our God the Almighty reigns, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Oportuit pati Christum, et resurgere a mortuis, et ita intrare in gloriam suam, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: The Christ had to suffer and rise from the dead, and so enter into his glory, alleluia.

title: WEDNESDAY In the Second Week of Easter

entrance_antiphon.latin: Confitebor tibi in populis, Domine, et narrabo nomen tuum fratribus meis, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: I will praise you, Lord, among the nations; I will tell of your name to my kin, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Dicit Dominus: Ego elegi vos de mundo et posui vos ut eatis, et fructum afferatis, et fructus vester maneat, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: I have chosen you from the world, says the Lord, and have appointed you to go and bear fruit, fruit that will last, alleluia.

title: THURSDAY In the Second Week of Easter

entrance_antiphon.latin: Deus, dum egredereris coram populo tuo, iter faciens eis, habitans in illis, terra mota est, caeli distillaverunt, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: O God, when you went forth before your people, marching with them and living among them, the earth trembled, heavens poured down rain, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Ecce ego vobiscum sum omnibus diebus, usque ad consummationem saeculi, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: Behold, I am with you always, even to the end of the age, alleluia.

title: FRIDAY In the Second Week of Easter

entrance_antiphon.latin: Redemisti, Domine, in sanguine tuo, ex omni tribu, et lingua, et populo, et natione, et fecisti nos Deo nostro regnum et sacerdotes, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: You have redeemed us, Lord, by your Blood, from every tribe and tongue and people and nation, and have made us into a kingdom, priests for our God, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Traditus est Christus Dominus noster propter delicta nostra, et resurrexit propter iustificationem nostram, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: Christ our Lord was handed over for our transgressions and was raised again for our justification, alleluia.

title: SATURDAY In the Second Week of Easter

entrance_antiphon.latin: Populus acquisitionis, annuntiate virtutes eius, qui vos de tenebris vocavit in admirabile lumen suum, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: O chosen people, proclaim the mighty works of him who called you out of darkness into his wonderful light, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Pater, quos dedisti mihi, volu ut ubi sum ego, et illi sint mecum, ut videant claritatem quam dedisti mihi, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: Father, I wish that, where I am, those you gave me may also be with me, that they may see the glory that you gave me, alleluia.

title: THIRD SUNDAY OF EASTER

entrance_antiphon.latin: Iubilate Deo, omnis terra, psalmum dicite nomini eius, date gloriam laudi eius, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: Cry out with joy to God, all the earth; O sing to the glory of his name. O render him glorious praise, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Cognoverunt discipuli Dominum Iesum in fractione panis, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: The disciples recognized the Lord Jesus in the breaking of the bread, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Oportebat Christum pati, et resurgere a mortuis tertia die, et praedicari in nomine eius paenitentiam, et remissionem peccatorum in omnes gentes, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: The Christ had to suffer and on the third day rise from the dead; in his name repentance and remission of sins must be preached to all the nations, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Dixit Iesus discipulis suis: Venite, prandete. Et accepit panem, et dedit eis, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: Jesus said to his disciples: Come and eat. And he took bread and gave it to them, alleluia.

title: MONDAY In the Third Week of Easter

entrance_antiphon.latin: Surrexit pastor bonus, qui animam suam posuit pro ovibus suis, et pro grege suo mori dignatus est, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: The Good Shepherd has risen, who laid down his life for his sheep and willingly died for his flock, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Pacem relinquo vobis, pacem meam do vobis; non quomodo mundus dat, ego do vobis, dicit Dominus, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: Peace I leave with you; my peace I give to you. Not as the world gives do I give it to you, says the Lord, alleluia.

title: TUESDAY In the Third Week of Easter

entrance_antiphon.latin: Laudem dicite Deo nostro, omnes qui timetis Deum, pusilli et magni, quia facta est salus, et virtus, et potestas Christi eius, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: Sing praise to our God, all you who fear God, both small and great, for now salvation and strength have come, and the power of his Christ, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Si mortui sumus cum Christo, credimus quia simul etiam vivemus cum Christo, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: If we have died with Christ, we believe that we shall also live with Christ, alleluia.

title: WEDNESDAY In the Third Week of Easter

entrance_antiphon.latin: Repleatur os meum laude tua, ut possim cantare; gaudebunt labia mea, dum cantavero tibi, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: Let my mouth be filled with your praise, that I may sing aloud; my lips shall shout for joy, when I sing to you, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Surrexit Dominus et illuxit nobis, quos redemit sanguine suo, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: The Lord has risen and shone his light upon us, whom he has redeemed by his Blood, alleluia.

title: THURSDAY In the Third Week of Easter

entrance_antiphon.latin: Cantemus Domino: gloriose enim magnificatus est. Fortitudo mea et laus mea Dominus, et factus est mihi in salutem, alleluia
entrance_antiphon.english: Let us sing to the Lord, for he has gloriously triumphed. The Lord is my strength and my might; he has become my salvation, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Pro omnibus mortuus est Christus, ut et qui vivunt iam non sibi vivant, sed ei qui pro ipsis mortuus est et resurrexit, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: Christ died for all, that those who live may live no longer for themselves, but for him, who died for them and is risen, alleluia.

title: FRIDAY In the Third Week of Easter

entrance_antiphon.latin: Dignus est Agnus, qui occisus est, accipere virtutem et divinitatem et sapientiam et fortitudinem et honorem, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: Worthy is the Lamb who was slain, to receive power and divinity, and wisdom and strength and honor, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Crucifixus surrexit a mortuis, et redemit nos, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: The Crucified is risen from the dead and has redeemed us, alleluia.

title: SATURDAY In the Third Week of Easter

entrance_antiphon.latin: Consepulti estis Christo in baptismo, in quo et resurrexistis per fidem operationis Dei, qui suscitavit illum a mortuis, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: You have been buried with Christ in Baptism, through which you also rose again by faith in the working of God, who raised him from the dead, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Pater, pro eis rogo, ut ipsi in nobis unum sint, ut credat mundus quia tu me misisti, dicit Dominus alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: Father, I pray for them, that they may be one in us, so that the world may believe it was you who sent me, says the Lord, alleluia.

title: FOURTH SUNDAY OF EASTER

entrance_antiphon.latin: Misericordia Domini plena est terra; verbo Domini caeli firmati sunt, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: The merciful love of the Lord fills the earth; by the word of the Lord the heavens were made, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Surrexit Pastor bonus, qui animam suam posuit pro ovibus suis, et pro grege suo mori dignatus est; alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: The Good Shepherd has risen, who laid down his life for his sheep and willingly died for his flock, alleluia.

title: MONDAY In the Fourth Week of Easter

entrance_antiphon.latin: Christus resurgens ex mortuis iam non moritur, mors illi ultra non dominabitur, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: Christ, having risen from the dead, dies now no more; death will no longer have dominion over him, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Stetit Iesus in medio discipulorum suorum, et dixit eis: Pax vobis, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: Jesus stood in the midst of his disciples and said to them: Peace be with you, alleluia.

title: TUESDAY In the Fourth Week of Easter

entrance_antiphon.latin: Gaudeamus et exsultemus et demus gloriam Deo, quoniam regnavit Dominus Deus noster omnipotens, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: Let us rejoice and be glad and give glory to God, for the Lord our God the Almighty reigns, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Oportuit pati Christum, et resurgere a mortuis, et ita intrare in gloriam suam, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: The Christ had to suffer and rise from the dead, and so enter into his glory, alleluia.

title: WEDNESDAY In the Fourth Week of Easter

entrance_antiphon.latin: Confitebor tibi in populis, Domine, et narrabo nomen tuum fratribus meis, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: I will praise you, Lord, among the nations; I will tell of your name to my kin, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Dicit Dominus: Ego elegi vos de mundo et posui vos ut eatis, et fructum afferatis, et fructus vester maneat, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: I have chosen you from the world, says the Lord, and have appointed you to go and bear fruit, fruit that will last, alleluia.

title: THURSDAY In the Fourth Week of Easter

entrance_antiphon.latin: Deus, dum egredereris coram populo tuo, iter faciens eis, habitans in illis, terra mota est, caeli distillaverunt, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: O God, when you went forth before your people, marching with them and living among them, the earth trembled, heavens poured down rain, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Ecce ego vobiscum sum omnibus diebus, usque ad consummationem saeculi, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: Behold, I am with you always, even to the end of the age, alleluia.

title: FRIDAY In the Fourth Week of Easter

entrance_antiphon.latin: Redemisti, Domine, in sanguine tuo, ex omni tribu, et lingua, et populo, et natione, et fecisti nos Deo nostro regnum et sacerdotes, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: You have redeemed us, Lord, by your Blood, from every tribe and tongue and people and nation, and have made us into a kingdom, priests for our God, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Traditus est Christus Dominus noster propter delicta nostra, et resurrexit propter iustificationem nostram, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: Christ our Lord was handed over for our transgressions and was raised again for our justification, alleluia.

title: SATURDAY In the Fourth Week of Easter

entrance_antiphon.latin: Populus acquisitionis, annuntiate virtutes eius, qui vos de tenebris vocavit in admirabile lumen suum, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: O chosen people, proclaim the mighty works of him who called you out of darkness into his wonderful light, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Pater, quos dedisti mihi, volu ut ubi sum ego, et illi sint mecum, ut videant claritatem quam dedisti mihi, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: Father, I wish that, where I am, those you gave me may also be with me, that they may see the glory that you gave me, alleluia.

title: FIFTH SUNDAY OF EASTER

entrance_antiphon.latin: Cantate Domino canticum novum, quia mirabilia fecit Dominus; ante conspectum gentium revelavit iustitiam suam, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: O sing a new song to the Lord, for he has worked wonders; in the sight of the nations he has shown his deliverance, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Ego sum vitis vera et vos palmites, dicit Dominus; qui manet in me et ego in eo, hic fert fructum multum, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: I am the true vine and you are the branches, says the Lord. Whoever remains in me, and I in him, bears fruit in plenty, alleluia.

title: MONDAY In the Fifth Week of Easter

entrance_antiphon.latin: Surrexit pastor bonus, qui animam suam posuit pro ovibus suis, et pro grege suo mori dignatus est, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: The Good Shepherd has risen, who laid down his life for his sheep and willingly died for his flock, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Pacem relinquo vobis, pacem meam do vobis; non quomodo mundus dat, ego do vobis, dicit Dominus, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: Peace I leave with you; my peace I give to you. Not as the world gives do I give it to you, says the Lord, alleluia.

title: TUESDAY In the Fifth Week of Easter

entrance_antiphon.latin: Laudem dicite Deo nostro, omnes qui timetis Deum, pusilli et magni, quia facta est salus, et virtus, et potestas Christi eius, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: Sing praise to our God, all you who fear God, both small and great, for now salvation and strength have come, and the power of his Christ, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Si mortui sumus cum Christo, credimus quia simul etiam vivemus cum Christo, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: If we have died with Christ, we believe that we shall also live with Christ, alleluia.

title: WEDNESDAY In the Fifth Week of Easter

entrance_antiphon.latin: Repleatur os meum laude tua, ut possim cantare; gaudebunt labia mea, dum cantavero tibi, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: Let my mouth be filled with your praise, that I may sing aloud; my lips shall shout for joy, when I sing to you, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Surrexit Dominus et illuxit nobis, quos redemit sanguine suo, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: The Lord has risen and shone his light upon us, whom he has redeemed by his Blood, alleluia.

title: THURSDAY In the Fifth Week of Easter

entrance_antiphon.latin: Cantemus Domino: gloriose enim magnificatus est. Fortitudo mea et laus mea Dominus, et factus est mihi in salutem, alleluia
entrance_antiphon.english: Let us sing to the Lord, for he has gloriously triumphed. The Lord is my strength and my might; he has become my salvation, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Pro omnibus mortuus est Christus, ut et qui vivunt iam non sibi vivant, sed ei qui pro ipsis mortuus est et resurrexit, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: Christ died for all, that those who live may live no longer for themselves, but for him, who died for them and is risen, alleluia.

title: FRIDAY In the Fifth Week of Easter

entrance_antiphon.latin: Dignus est Agnus, qui occisus est, accipere virtutem et divinitatem et sapientiam et fortitudinem et honorem, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: Worthy is the Lamb who was slain, to receive power and divinity, and wisdom and strength and honor, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Crucifixus surrexit a mortuis, et redemit nos, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: The Crucified is risen from the dead and has redeemed us, alleluia.

title: SATURDAY In the Fifth Week of Easter

entrance_antiphon.latin: Consepulti estis Christo in baptismo, in quo et resurrexistis per fidem operationis Dei, qui suscitavit illum a mortuis, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: You have been buried with Christ in Baptism, through which you also rose again by faith in the working of God, who raised him from the dead, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Pater, pro eis rogo, ut ipsi in nobis unum sint, ut credat mundus quia tu me misisti, dicit Dominus alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: Father, I pray for them, that they may be one in us, so that the world may believe it was you who sent me, says the Lord, alleluia.

title: SIXTH SUNDAY OF EASTER

entrance_antiphon.latin: Vocem iucunditatis annuntiate, et audiatur, annuntiate usque ad extremum terrae; liberavit Dominus populum suum, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: Proclaim a joyful sound and let it be heard; proclaim to the ends of the earth: The Lord has freed his people, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Si diligitus me, mandata mea servate, dicit Dominus. Et ego rogabo Patrem, et alium Paraclitum dabit vobis, ut maneat vobiscum in aeternum, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: If you love me, keep my commandments, says the Lord, and I will ask the Father and he will send you another Paraclete, to abide with you for ever, alleluia.

title: MONDAY In the Sixth Week of Easter

entrance_antiphon.latin: Christus resurgens ex mortuis iam non moritur, mors illi ultra non dominabitur, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: Christ, having risen from the dead, dies now no more; death will no longer have dominion over him, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Stetit Iesus in medio discipulorum suorum, et dixit eis: Pax vobis, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: Jesus stood in the midst of his disciples and said to them: Peace be with you, alleluia.

title: TUESDAY In the Sixth Week of Easter

entrance_antiphon.latin: Gaudeamus et exsultemus et demus gloriam Deo, quoniam regnavit Dominus Deus noster omnipotens, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: Let us rejoice and be glad and give glory to God, for the Lord our God the Almighty reigns, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Oportuit pati Christum, et resurgere a mortuis, et ita intrare in gloriam suam, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: The Christ had to suffer and rise from the dead, and so enter into his glory, alleluia.

title: WEDNESDAY In the Sixth Week of Easter

entrance_antiphon.latin: Confitebor tibi in populis, Domine, et narrabo nomen tuum fratribus meis, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: I will praise you, Lord, among the nations; I will tell of your name to my kin, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Dicit Dominus: Ego elegi vos de mundo et posui vos ut eatis, et fructum afferatis, et fructus vester maneat, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: I have chosen you from the world, says the Lord, and have appointed you to go and bear fruit, fruit that will last, alleluia.

title: At the Vigil Mass

entrance_antiphon.latin: Regna terrae, cantate Deo, psallita Domino, qui ascendit super caelum caeli; magnificentia et virtus eius in nubibus, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: You kingdoms of the earth, sing to God; praise the Lord, who ascends above the highest heavens; his majesty and might are in the skies, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Christus, unam pro peccatis offerens hostiam, in sempiternum sedet in dextera Dei, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: Christ, offering a single sacrifice for sins, is seated for ever at God's right hand, alleluia.

title: At the Mass during the Day

entrance_antiphon.latin: Viri Galilaei, quid admiramini ascipientes in caelum? Quemadmodum vidistis eum ascendentum in caelum, ita veniet, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: Men of Galilee, why gaze in wonder at the heavens? This Jesus whom you saw ascending into heaven will return as you saw him go, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Ecce ego vobiscum sum omnibus diebus, usque ad consummationem saeculi, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: Behold, I am with you always, even to the end of the age, alleluia.

title: THURSDAY In the Sixth Week of Easter

entrance_antiphon.latin: Deus, dum egredereris coram populo tuo, iter faciens eis, habitans in illis, terra mota est, caeli distillaverunt, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: O God, when you went forth before your people, marching with them and living among them, the earth trembled, heavens poured down rain, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Ecce ego vobiscum sum omnibus diebus, usque ad consummationem saeculi, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: Behold, I am with you always, even to the end of the age, alleluia.

title: FRIDAY In the Sixth Week of Easter

entrance_antiphon.latin: Redemisti, Domine, in sanguine tuo, ex omni tribu, et lingua, et populo, et natione, et fecisti nos Deo nostro regnum et sacerdotes, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: You have redeemed us, Lord, by your Blood, from every tribe and tongue and people and nation, and have made us into a kingdom, priests for our God, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Traditus est Christus Dominus noster propter delicta nostra, et resurrexit propter iustificationem nostram, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: Christ our Lord was handed over for our transgressions and was raised again for our justification, alleluia.

title: SATURDAY In the Sixth Week of Easter

entrance_antiphon.latin: Populus acquisitionis, annuntiate virtutes eius, qui vos de tenebris vocavit in admirabile lumen suum, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: O chosen people, proclaim the mighty works of him who called you out of darkness into his wonderful light, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Pater, quos dedisti mihi, volu ut ubi sum ego, et illi sint mecum, ut videant claritatem quam dedisti mihi, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: Father, I wish that, where I am, those you gave me may also be with me, that they may see the glory that you gave me, alleluia.

title: SEVENTH SUNDAY OF EASTER

entrance_antiphon.latin: Exaudi, Domine, vocem meam, qua clamavi as te. Tibi dixit cor meum, quaesivi vultum tuum requiram; ne avertas faciem tuam a me, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: O Lord, hear my voice, for I have called to you; of you my heart has spoken: Seek his face; hide not your face from me, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Rogo, Pater, ut sint unum, sicut et nos unum sumus, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: Father, I pray that they may be one as we also are one, alleluia.

title: MONDAY In the Seventh Week of Easter

entrance_antiphon.latin: Accipietis virtutem supervenientis Spiritus Sancti in vos, et eritis mihi testes usque ad ultimum terrae, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: You will receive the power of the Holy Spirit coming upon you, and you will be my witnesses, even to the ends of the earth, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Non vos relinquam orphanos, dicit Dominus; veniam ad vos iterum, et gaudebit cor vestrum, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: I will not leave you orphans, says the Lord; I will come to you again, and your heart will rejoice, alleluia.

title: TUESDAY In the Seventh Week of Easter

entrance_antiphon.latin: Ego sum primus et novissimus; et vivus et fui mortuus, et ecce sum vivens in saecula saeculorum, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: I am the first and the last, I was dead and am now alive. Behold, I am alive for ever and ever, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Spiritus Sanctus, quem mittet Pater in nomine meo ille vos docebit omnia, et suggeret vobis omnia quaecumque dixero vobis, dicit Dominus.
communion_antiphon.english: The Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in my name, will teach you all things and remind you of all I have told you, says the Lord, alleluia.

title: WEDNESDAY In the Seventh Week of Easter

entrance_antiphon.latin: Omnes gentes, plaudite manibus, iubiliate Deo in voce exsultationis, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: All peoples, clap your hands. Cry to God with shouts of joy, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Cum venerit Paraclitus, quem ego mittam vobis, Spiritum veritatis qui a Patre procedit, ille testimonium perhibebit de me, et vos testimonium perhibebitis, dicit Dominus, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: When the Paraclete comes, whom I will send you, the Spirit of Truth who proceeds from the Father, he will bear witness to me, and you also will bear witness, says the Lord, alleluia.

title: THURSDAY In the Seventh Week of Easter

entrance_antiphon.latin: Adeamus cum fiducia ad thronum gratiae, ut misericordiam consequamur, et gratiam inveniamus in auxilio opportuno, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: With boldness let us approach the throne of grace, that we may receive mercy and find grace as a timely help, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Ego veritatem dico vobis, expedit vobis ut ego vadam; si enim non abiero, Paraclitus non veniet ad vos, dicit Dominus, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: I tell you the truth, it is for your good that I go; for if I do not go away, the Paraclete will not come to you, says the Lord, alleluia.

title: FRIDAY In the Seventh Week of Easter

entrance_antiphon.latin: Dilexit nos Christus, et lavit nos a peccatis nostris in sanguine suo, et fecit nos regnum et sacerdotes Deo et Patri suo, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: Christ loved us and washed us clean of our sins by his Blood, and made us into a kingdom, priests for his God and Father, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Cum venerit Spiritus veritatis, docebit vos omnem veritatem, dicit Dominus, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: When the Spirit of truth comes, he will teach you all truth, says the Lord, alleluia.

title: SATURDAY In the Seventh Week of Easter

entrance_antiphon.latin: Erant discipuli perseverantes unanimiter in oratione, cum mulieribus et Maria Matre Iesu, et fratribus eius, alleluia.
entrance_antiphon.english: The disciples devoted themselves with one accord to prayer with the women, and Mary the Mother of Jesus, and his brethren, alleluia.

communion_antiphon.latin: Spiritus Sanctus, ille me clarificabit, quia de meo accipiet, et annuntiabit vobis, dicit Dominus, alleluia.
communion_antiphon.english: The Holy Spirit will glorify me, for he will take from what is mine and declare it to you, says the Lord, alleluia.
